author: jmurray-dev
body: Note to self: revisit whether the worker should pull from a queue instead of polling the DB.

author: jmurray-dev
body: Switched the hashing notes from bcrypt to Argon2id after reading the OWASP guidance.

author: priya.chandr
body: Token rotation cadence looks solid. Maybe add a line about refresh token reuse detection?

author: jmurray-dev
body: Added a composite index on (user_id, created_at) after the bookmark list query got slow.

author: jmurray-dev
body: Tested this end to end after the node-2 outage. etcd snapshot restore worked on the first try.

author: jmurray-dev
body: Reminder: run the quarterly restore drill. Last one was clean but I rushed the WAL replay step.

author: dave.kowalczyk
body: Blue/green via selector swap is clever for a homelab. Watch out for in-flight sessions during the cutover.

author: jmurray-dev
body: Good point. Added a 30s drain before flipping the Service selector.

author: jmurray-dev
body: The chapter on consensus finally made Raft click for me. Mapping it to etcd in the homelab helped.

author: jmurray-dev
body: slog made structured logging painless. Dropped my custom logger wrapper entirely.

author: sofia.reyes.dev
body: I can have the landing page mockup ready before the hackathon kickoff. Will share the Figma link.

author: daniel.okafor
body: Happy to run the data track. Can we lock the dataset by end of week?

author: jmurray-dev
body: The duplicate-charge bug was a missing idempotency key on retries. Fixed and added a webhook dedupe table.

author: jmurray-dev
body: Algolia free tier is plenty for now. Postgres fallback kicks in only on a cold index.

author: jmurray-dev
body: Root cause: container logs never rotated. Added log-rotate to the node image and a Longhorn space alert.

author: tomas.alvarez.dev
body: The heap profile walkthrough is really clear. Borrowing this approach for our service at work.

author: jmurray-dev
body: Moved remote state locking to DynamoDB after a concurrent apply clobbered the plan once.

author: jmurray-dev
body: DNS-01 with Cloudflare is more reliable than HTTP-01 behind the tunnel. Renewal failures dropped to zero.

author: priya.chandr
body: Path versioning is the right call for a small client surface. Deprecation policy is sensible.

author: jmurray-dev
body: Wired PagerDuty so only Sev1 pages me after hours. Everything else waits for the morning.
